baseline_refresh_policy: A baseline is only replaced with reviewed evidence: linked before/after reports, the reason for the change (an approved optimization, an accepted regression trade-off, or a runner-class migration), and explicit reviewer sign-off in the PR description. No automatic or silent widening — see tests/performance/README.md#baseline-update-review-requirements.
owner: See CODEOWNERS / PR reviewer for crates/repose-core/benches and tests/performance.

exit_code.reason: Semantic contract, not a measurement; any change is a correctness regression.

command_count.reason: Deterministic for mock-kind workloads; a change means the algorithm now issues a different number of remote commands.

stdout_digest.reason: Deterministic for mock-kind workloads; a change means observable output changed.

peak_concurrency.reason: Candidate must not exceed baseline's observed peak in-flight operations; concurrency can drop (fan-out becoming more serial is caught by the fleet_add/fleet_install Criterion assertions and repose-core::mock's own gated tests, not by this numeric comparison alone).

latency_ns.p50.evidence: 5 independent local runs of mock-add-100h (20 reps, 3 warmup) on this shared/uncontrolled runner ranged 189625-296375 ns p50 (~56% spread); mock-add-1h ranged 33708-44916 ns (~33% spread). A 100% (2x) threshold sits comfortably above observed noise while still catching a real regression. Tighten per-runner-class once a controlled/scheduled runner has its own variance study (see baseline_refresh_policy).

latency_ns.p95.evidence: Same measurement set as latency_ns.p50; p95 showed comparable relative spread across the 5 runs.

latency_ns.p99.evidence: Tail percentile from a 20-repetition sample is the noisiest single number collected; a slightly wider margin than p50/p95 avoids flagging sampling noise as a regression.

throughput_ops_per_sec.evidence: Derived from latency_ns.p50 (throughput = host_count / p50_seconds); the ratio is the inverse of the p50 tolerance above.

peak_rss_bytes.evidence: 3 independent runs of mock-add-100h RSS ranged 7307264-7389184 bytes (~1.1% spread) — far tighter than latency. 20% leaves headroom for legitimate allocator/runtime variation while still catching a material memory regression.
